name: Low User Adoption
signals: great demos but reps stopped using it
description: The pilot launched but real users disengaged. The tool was not woven into daily workflows, onboarding was thin, and there was no habit-forming loop, so usage decayed after the initial novelty.

name: Unclear ROI / Value
description: Success was never tied to a measurable business outcome. With no baseline or KPI defined up front, leadership cannot quantify return and the pilot stalls when budget renewal time comes.

name: Poor Data Quality
description: The model is fed incomplete, inconsistent, or siloed data. Output quality tracks input quality, so unreliable data undermines trust regardless of model capability.

name: Governance & Compliance Blockers
signals: no approval to go to production
description: Security, legal, or compliance review was engaged too late. Unresolved privacy, audit, and policy questions prevent the pilot from reaching production.

name: Scope Creep / Unclear Requirements
description: The pilot tried to solve too much at once with shifting, ambiguous requirements. Without a bounded scope and definition of done, it never converges to a shippable result.

name: Latency & Cost at Scale
description: The solution worked in a demo but response times and per-request cost become untenable at production volume, so it cannot scale economically.

name: Hallucination & Trust Erosion
description: The model produces confident but incorrect output. A few visible errors erode user trust permanently, and without grounding or guardrails users stop relying on it.

name: Change-Management Resistance
description: The human side was neglected. Without an executive sponsor, internal champions, and clear communication about how roles change, staff resist and quietly undermine the rollout.

name: Integration & Production Gap
description: The prototype lives in isolation and cannot be wired into existing systems (CRM, ERP, data pipelines). The last-mile engineering to reach production was underestimated.

name: Poor Use-Case Fit
description: The pilot was chosen for novelty rather than a real, high-value user need. The use case is a poor fit for the technology, so even a technically successful build delivers little value.
